what_it_does: Exports PanelView, a React component that renders a declarative panel from a definition object p: it shows a title, iterates over p.fields, renders a label and either a controlled select (when f.type is 'select') or an uncontrolled input with defaultValue and onBlur (otherwise), and shows 'no fields' when the field list is absent or empty.
observations: A comment says this component was carved from App.tsx:166-186 and exists so render-prone declarative-panel work lives inside a component that can be wrapped by PanelErrorBoundary.
It renders a generic panel using p.title and p.fields with no knowledge of specific panel semantics.
Field type 'select' uses a controlled select element; every other type falls back to an input using defaultValue and onBlur.
When p.fields is missing or empty it renders a 'no fields' placeholder.
Types are all any: p, f, value callback argument, etc.
standouts: Explicit comment framing the component as a fault-containment boundary for declarative rendering.
Mixed input strategy: controlled select versus uncontrolled defaultValue/onBlur input.
Generic any-typed schema-driven rendering with no explicit interfaces.
conventions: Functional React component with destructured props.
key props supplied on mapped fields and select options.
CSS class names prefixed with op- (op-panel, op-title, op-field, op-label).
concerns: Using defaultValue with onBlur means text inputs will not reflect external value changes after initial mount.
Unknown field types silently fall back to a plain input.
Heavy use of any hides the panel/field contract and removes compile-time safety.
No validation or guard for malformed p structures.
notes: A generic declarative panel renderer that deliberately localizes render-time failures and uses a controlled select but uncontrolled-on-blur text inputs.
questions: What is the exact schema or interface for p and for each field f?
Where is PanelErrorBoundary defined and how does it wrap PanelView?
Are additional field types planned beyond 'select'?
Why is defaultValue/onBlur preferred over a fully controlled input for text fields?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the comment states the component isolates render-prone declarative panel rendering so errors are caught by a boundary, and the code does exactly that.
intention_signals: Provide a small, replaceable renderer for declarative operation panels so that malformed panel definitions fail inside a boundary rather than crashing the surrounding application shell.
novelty: The file's comment frames the component as an intentional fault-containment boundary for declarative rendering, and it pairs a controlled select with an uncontrolled-on-blur input strategy.
contribution: A compact, schema-driven panel renderer that keeps panel-rendering failures local and gives the fused system a minimal, boundary-friendly field-rendering primitive.
summary_for_embedding: PanelView.tsx exports a React component that renders a declarative panel definition with title, select/input fields, and a no-fields fallback, designed to be wrapped by an error boundary.
intention_for_embedding: To isolate render-time failures of a generic declarative panel so they are caught by a local error boundary instead of the parent shell.

symbols.does: Renders a declarative panel from a definition object, dispatching fields to a controlled select or an uncontrolled input.
symbols.description: PanelView is an exported React functional component that receives a panel definition p, a value accessor, and an onSet callback. It renders p.title and each field in p.fields, producing a controlled select for f.type === 'select' and a defaultValue input with onBlur for all other types, with a 'no fields' fallback when the field list is absent or empty.
